records.ety: [L. convenire; con- + venire to come: cf. F. convenir to agree, to be fitting, OF. also, to assemble. See Come, and cf. Covenant.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Convened (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Convenong.]

defs: To come together; to meet; to unite.
quotes: In shortsighted men . . . the rays converge and convene in the eyes before they come at the bottom.

defs: To come together, as in one body or for a public purpose; to meet; to assemble.
quotes: The Parliament of Scotland now convened.
Faint, underneath, the household fowls convene.

defs: To cause to assemble; to call together; to convoke.
quotes: And now the almighty father of the gods Convenes a council in the blest abodes.

defs: To summon judicially to meet or appear.
quotes: By the papal canon law, clerks . . . can not be convened before any but an ecclesiastical judge.
